visible_observations: A crouched bat-like humanoid aims or braces an enormous crescent bow grown from veined membrane and hooked bone, with several taut cords converging across the chest and face.

The body and face split into pale and black halves; two very large unequal ears, a skull-like nose, narrow eyes, bared teeth, fur, clawed hands, and digitigrade feet establish a predatory nonhuman anatomy.
One lifted knee, a low planted leg, a forward shoulder, and hands engaged with the living bow create compressed attack tension.
strengths: The asymmetrical black-and-pale body, giant ears, crouched archer posture, and membrane-bone bow combine species and weapon into a singular combat identity.
Dry fur, leathery membrane, pitted bone, cords, and scarred skin are materially differentiated and avoid clean decorative magic.
defects: Multiple bow cords cross the mouth, eyes, torso, and arms, obscuring the action mechanics and making the face harder to read at full size.
The hooked bow occupies much of the outline and can overpower the creature's own body plan, while the dense overlap of limbs, membrane, and strings reduces anatomical clarity.
The bared teeth and narrowed eyes communicate a familiar monster scowl more clearly than an individual emotional contradiction.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot determine which hand draws the weapon, what serves as the projectile, or whether the bow is an attached limb, separate organism, or carried tool.
The split coloration reads clearly, but its biological or supernatural cause is not visually certain.

visible_observations: Three black alternatives show a hunched figure working inside a tall recurved bow, a frontal long-robed four-armed creature with clawed hands, and a long-legged side-facing archer extending a large membrane bow.
The candidates change weapon dependence, arm count, posture, height, locomotion, negative space, and the balance between creature and garment mass.
strengths: Candidates A and C use the huge bow to create strong open negative space and a clear ranged-action line, while candidate B offers a genuinely different many-armed occult body plan.
Large pointed ears and clawed extremities keep a nonhuman read across all three options.
defects: Candidate A folds body and bow into a congested left-heavy cluster, candidate B loses the ranged identity entirely, and candidate C lets the bow rival the body in visual importance.
The three options do not preserve one stable anatomical premise: limb count, garment mass, and bow integration shift enough to read as different creatures.
uncertainties: The sheet does not clarify whether the split-color anatomy or living membrane construction was present in each silhouette option.

visible_observations: The pale-and-black face is pitched forward between huge ears, with both narrow eyes fixed toward the viewer, brows compressed, nostril cavity dark, and small sharp teeth exposed in a tight horizontal grin or snarl.
Taut cords cut diagonally across the forehead, mouth, shoulder, and chest, connecting facial tension directly to the drawn weapon posture.
strengths: Direct eye fixation, lowered head, tightened mouth, split anatomy, and weapon tension communicate active predatory concentration rather than neutral posing.
The unequal coloration and ears make the face immediately individual even within a familiar bat-like species family.
defects: The cords partially mask the eyes and mouth, limiting precise interpretation of asymmetry and expression.
Narrowed eyes plus exposed teeth resolve mainly as generic feral aggression; no distinct want, fear, restraint, or manipulative relationship is visible beyond imminent attack.
uncertainties: The mouth could be a grin, snarl, or tension from pulling a cord, and the image does not establish whether the viewer is prey or merely aligned with the weapon aim.

visible_observations: At native size, the hooked crescent bow, two large ears, low crouch, pale left side, dark right side, and raised knee remain visible.
The face, teeth, individual cords, fur, claws, exact hand positions, and most internal membrane holes collapse into the overlapping bow-and-body mass.
strengths: The arcing weapon and tall ears produce a recognizable bat-archer outline with more lateral reach than a standard humanoid figure.
The pale-dark split survives enough to reinforce asymmetry inside the silhouette.
defects: Bow, strings, ears, and limbs overlap around the center, making the attack pose difficult to parse at the frozen scale.
Recognition depends strongly on the oversized bow; the creature's facial threat and specific split anatomy do not remain independently readable.
uncertainties: The native image alone does not establish whether players would perceive an archer preparing a shot or a crouched winged creature enclosed by a large appendage.
